idea.hook: How much EMPTY is BETWEEN galaxies?
facts: Our home, the Milky Way galaxy, is an immense disk about 100,000 light-years wide.
But the average distance to our nearest large galactic neighbor (Andromeda) is 2.5 MILLION light-years.
idea.payoff: That means the void between galaxies is 25 TIMES wider than an entire galaxy itself! Space is overwhelmingly empty, even in our cosmic neighborhood.
idea.title: Galaxy Gaps: Mostly Empty Space! 😱🌌

scenes.text: How much EMPTY is BETWEEN galaxies?
scenes.visual: Starts with a single bright star, rapidly zooms out to reveal vast, dark cosmic emptiness.
An infographic illustration of a flat spiral galaxy (Milky Way). A measurement line appears across it with '100,000 light-years'.
The Milky Way graphic, now with countless tiny twinkling dots representing stars, and numbers rapidly counting up to '400 BILLION'.
Two large spiral galaxies (Milky Way and Andromeda) float in immense darkness, separated by a vast distance. A glowing dashed line connects them with '2.5 MILLION light-years'.

scenes.text: Void is 25x WIDER than a galaxy! Space is overwhelmingly EMPTY.
scenes.visual: The two galaxies are now much smaller, emphasizing the gigantic, dark void between them. A large, glowing '25x' pulses in the empty space, reinforcing the scale.
